items.briefing:
A new survey by the Office of the Australian Information Commissioner (OAIC) finds Australians are increasingly concerned about privacy, with trust in AI remaining low. This reflects a growing awareness that personal data — including that held by training organisations — needs careful protection. Separately, a Security Brief Australia article highlights that cyber risk in education now extends well beyond campus, meaning staff working remotely or students accessing systems from home are also potential entry points for attackers.

**Practical tip:** Remind staff and students to use strong, unique passwords for any learning platform or work system they access from home.

featured.title: Cyber risk in education now extends far beyond the school gate
featured.quote: Education sector vendor breaches expose how learning platforms extend cyber risks beyond school gates.
